description: The cottage is open, the fire still warm, and the meal on the table half-eaten. Someone left here in a hurry — and someone is coming up the path behind you.
objective: Decide what to tell the Overseer before she reaches the door.
customIntroduction: The door is open and the fire still warm. A bowl on the table holds half a meal; a chair is on its side. Whatever happened here happened in the last hour. Then through the window: three figures on the eastern path, walking unhurried — a tall elf in a Concordat collar, flanked by two cloaked enforcers. They have not seen you yet. They are coming to this cottage.
customContext: SETTING CUE: The cottage's previous occupant was an elf hiding from the Quota — an unlicensed birth eight months ago, the kind of correction the Concordat is paid to find. She fled this morning when a neighbour warned her the Concordat was on the road; the half-finished Passage necklace on the table by the hearth is hers, woven for a child whose existence is the entire reason any of this is happening. INVESTIGATION OPPORTUNITIES inside the cottage (use request_ability_check with skill perception, investigation, or arcana): a charcoal scrawl on a hearth-stone in elven script reading "south by the broken aqueduct"; a hidden compartment under the floor with three more half-woven necklaces; a discarded forged Concordat travel permit with the date torn off; the warm meal itself implies the occupant cannot have gone far. APPROACH FROM THE PATH: the Concordat Overseer (npc id "concordat_overseer") and her two enforcers (two bandit npc ids — represent them as Concordat-employed mundane thugs, not common road bandits) walk up the east path; they will reach the cottage door in about ninety seconds of in-fiction time. THEY START NEUTRAL. The Overseer expects to find the elf at home; if she does not, she will question whoever IS at home with cold civility. Her primary lever is suspicion that the player is sheltering a fugitive. ROLEPLAY HER as selfish, paranoid, and personally afraid: every day she does not close her count brings her own execution closer. She will lie, threaten, or offer surprising clemency depending on which serves her. RESOLUTION PATHS: (a) Tell her the truth and turn the elf in via the southern aqueduct — she gains a fix on the fugitive; the player walks; set_world_flag with name "stillweir_elf_betrayed" and value true. (b) Lie convincingly (Deception vs her passive Insight 13) — she leaves to search the western path; the elf has a chance to disappear; set_world_flag with name "stillweir_elf_aided" and value true. (c) Refuse to speak — she calls the enforcers in; set_disposition on the overseer and both enforcers to enemy and trigger_combat. (d) Hide in the cottage — Stealth vs her passive Perception 13; if hidden, the Overseer searches briefly and departs; the player can still pursue the elf themselves. CHAPTER COMPLETION: set_world_flag empty_hearth_resolved=true on any resolution.
